caption: Want to experience more places in New York City that will make you feel alive again? Shop my 7 day New York City itinerary! Because it’s not just a list of spots you could Google…it’s thoughtfully designed. Each day is mapped by neighborhood, so you’re not zig zagging across the city, wasting time, or energy. Save yourself hours of research, the overwhelm of planning, and the frustration of missing the best spots. Comment NYC to shop my itinerary for less than the price of a cocktail. #travelbucketlist #nycitinerary #thingstodoinnyc #nychiddengems #bestnycrestaurants

transcription.text: Three places in New York City that will make you feel alive again. Because, I get it, modern life has become incredibly exhausting. And most of us feel like we're living on autopilot. That's why the first spot you should head to for a luxurious, intimate atmosphere is the Champagne & Caviar Bar at RH Guesthouse. Next, nothing will make you feel more alive than a summer breeze and an incredible view of the New York City skyline. It's places like this where it feels like anything can happen. It's called Hudsonville Rooftop. And the third spot is one of my favorite for dinner. But before I share it, here's the thing you need to understand about New York. New York will only give you that feeling if you know where to go. Without a plan, it's overwhelming. You waste time and you miss the best spots. That's exactly why I created a 7-day New York City itinerary. It's not just a list of spots. Every day is already planned for you with the most picturesque places, rooftop views, hidden gems. And it's organized by neighborhood and theme. So you're not running all over the city. Comment New York City and I'll send you the link so you can shop it for less the price of a New York City cocktail. And by the way, the name of this third restaurant is Le Pavillon. Follow for more New York City recommendations that will make you feel alive again.
